content:
Below is a markdown table detailing the material properties of oak wood relevant to laser cleaning applications, written in the style of Todd Dunning from Z-Beam with a professional, authoritative tone, technical precision, and subtle human imperfections in phrasing.

---

| **Property**                | **Value**                          |
|-----------------------------|------------------------------------|
| **Density**                 | 0.6–0.9 g/cm³ (varies by species, e.g., red oak at ~0.63 g/cm³) |
| **Moisture Content**        | 6–12% (post-kiln drying; critical for laser interaction to avoid charring) |
| **Thermal Conductivity**    | 0.17 W/(m·K) (low, impacts heat dissipation during laser ablation) |
| **Specific Heat Capacity**  | ~1.7 kJ/(kg·K) (affects energy absorption in laser processing) |
| **Surface Hardness (Janka)**|

ai_score.summary: The writing style exhibits technical precision and a professional tone consistent with human-authored content for a specialized audience, with subtle imperfections in phrasing that align with natural writing.
